```{r model validation prevalence, eval=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_x) + 
                                  offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)), 
                                family = nbinom12, data = hurricane_df_prevalence)

#nbinom12 sqrt

nbinom12sqrtoff <- glmmTMB(psite_prevalence ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + 
                                  (1|fish_sp_x) + offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)),
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_x) + 
                                  offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)), 
                                family = nbinom12(link = "sqrt"), data = hurricane_df_prevalence)



# List of all model objects
models <- list(
  nbinom1log, nbinom1sqrt, nbinom2log, nbinom2sqrt, nbinom12log, nbinom12sqrt,
  nbinom1logoff, nbinom1sqrtoff, nbinom2logoff, nbinom2sqrtoff,
  nbinom12logoff, nbinom12sqrtoff
)

# Model names
model_names <- c(
  "nbinom1log", "nbinom1sqrt", "nbinom2log", "nbinom2sqrt", "nbinom12log", "nbinom12sqrt",
  "nbinom1logoff", "nbinom1sqrtoff", "nbinom2logoff", "nbinom2sqrtoff",
  "nbinom12logoff", "nbinom12sqrtoff"
)

# Simulate residuals and extract diagnostics
residual_summaries <- map2_df(models, model_names, function(model, name) {
  sim <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = model, plot = FALSE)
  data.frame(
    model = name,
    dispersion = testDispersion(sim)$p.value,
    uniformity = testUniformity(sim)$p.value,
    outliers = testOutliers(sim)$p.value,
    AIC = AIC(model)
  )
})

# View and sort by AIC (lower is better) and test stats (higher p-values indicate better fit)
residual_summaries %>%
  arrange(AIC) %>%
  print()

```

## Final Hurricane Prevalence Model
```{r}
# prevalence
hurricane_prevalence_final <- glmmTMB(psite_prevalence ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_x) + offset(log(total_length_mm)),
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_x) + offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)), 
                                family = nbinom2, data = hurricane_df_prevalence)

simulateResiduals(fittedModel = hurricane_prevalence_final, plot = TRUE)
summary(hurricane_prevalence_final)
plot(parameters(hurricane_prevalence_final))
```

switch things around so that monogenes are not reference category? how do i get stats on the reference level?

## Plots -- hurricane prevalence
## Hurricance prevalence prediction plot
```{r}
#### prevalence plot 

p1p <- ggpredict(hurricane_prevalence_final, terms = c("event"))

p1p <- p1p %>%
  rename(
    event = x,
    estimate = predicted,
    se = std.error,
    lower = conf.low,
    upper = conf.high
  ) %>%
  mutate(psite_type = "myxozoan") %>%
  filter(!is.na(estimate), !is.na(lower), !is.na(upper))

hpp_f <- ggplot(p1p,
         aes(x = event, y = estimate)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, group = psite_type),
                width = 0.05,
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = psite_type, linetype = psite_type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = psite_type, group = psite_type),
             pch = 21, size = 5,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("myxozoan" = "#07c3c5")) +
  labs(x = "Event", y = "Estimated Parasite Prevalence", fill = "Parasite Type", linetype = "Parasite Type") +
  connor_theme
```

PLot prevalence model and raw data together 
```{r}

```

## Zero Inflated prevalence plots
```{r}
azi1 <- ggemmeans(hurricane_prevalence_final, terms = c("event"), component = "zi")

azi1 <- azi1 %>%
  rename(
    event = x,
    estimate = predicted,
    se = std.error,
    lower = conf.low,
    upper = conf.high,
    psite_type = group
  ) %>%
  mutate(psite_type = ifelse(psite_type == "1", "myxozoan", psite_type)) %>%
  drop_na()

azi1p <- ggplot(azi1,
       aes(x = event, y = estimate)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, group = event),
                width = 0.05,
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = event, group = event),
             pch = 21, size = 5,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("drought" = "#D84D16", "flood" = "#1BB6AF","control" = "#F4B95A", "impact" = "#FF69B4")) + 
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5), breaks = seq(0, 5, 2)) +
  labs(x = "Event", y = "Zero Inflation Estimate", fill = "Event") +
  connor_theme

df_prevalence_plot_zi <- ggarrange(pzi1p, common.legend = FALSE, legend = "bottom", labels = c("A"))
plot(df_prevalence_plot_zi)

```
## Plot Zi and conditional model outputs together -- abundance
```{r}
hurricance_prevalence_plot_total <- ggarrange(hpp_f, pzi1p, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom", labels = c("A", "B"))
plot(hurricance_prevalence_plot_total)
```


# Estimating Biodiversity Changes
Jackknife estimates - all parasites
```{r}
# create DF for biodiversity estimates -- drop species that have very low replication, so kick out all cilliate, nematomorph, leech, copepod observations (but lets keep in nematodes, cestodes, and acanths)
# Remove unwanted parasite types and drop their factor levels
biodiv_df <- hurricane_df6 %>% 
  filter(!psite_type %in% c("nematomorph", "ciliate", "leech", "copepod")) %>% 
  droplevels()

# Get all unique combinations of grouping variables
group_combos <- unique(biodiv_df[, c("event", "timing", "ci", "psite_type", "fish_sp_x",  "psite_spp_y")])

# Empty results data frame
jackknife_results <- data.frame(
  event = character(),
  timing = character(),
  ci = character(),
  psite_type = character(),
  fish_sp_x = character(),
  psite_spp_y = character(),
  jackknife_mean_richness = numeric(),
  total_psite_count = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Loop over each combination
for (i in 1:nrow(group_combos)) {
  event <- group_combos$event[i]
  timing <- group_combos$timing[i]
  ci <- group_combos$ci[i]
  psite <- group_combos$psite_type[i]
  fish <- group_combos$fish_sp_x[i]
  rich <- group_combos$psite_spp_y[i]
  
  # Safe subsetting using which()
  sub_df <- biodiv_df[which(
    biodiv_df$event == event &
    biodiv_df$timing == timing &
    biodiv_df$ci == ci &
    biodiv_df$psite_type == psite &
    biodiv_df$fish_sp_x == fish &
    biodiv_df$psite_spp_y == rich
  ), ]
  
  # Remove 0-counts
  sub_df <- sub_df[sub_df$psite_count != 0, ]
  
  # Skip if no data
  if (nrow(sub_df) == 0) next
  
  # Unique samples
  sample_ids <- unique(sub_df$x)
  richness_values <- numeric(length(sample_ids))
  
  for (j in seq_along(sample_ids)) {
    id_to_leave_out <- sample_ids[j]
    df_minus_one <- sub_df[sub_df$x != id_to_leave_out, ]
    richness_values[j] <- length(unique(df_minus_one$psite_spp_x))
  }
  
  total_count <- sum(sub_df$psite_count, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  jackknife_results <- rbind(jackknife_results, data.frame(
    event = event,
    timing = timing,
    ci = ci,
    psite_type = as.character(psite), 
    fish_sp_x = as.character(fish),
    psite_spp_y = as.character(rich),
    jackknife_mean_richness = mean(richness_values),
    total_psite_count = total_count
  ))
}

# Remove incomplete rows
jackknife_results <- jackknife_results[complete.cases(jackknife_results), ]


# create plots for the variety of combinations of factors we want to investigate

# event
ggplot(jackknife_results,
       aes(x = psite_type, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(timing ~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme
```

## Plot jacknife estimates
```{r}
ggplot(jackknife_results,
       aes(x = psite_type, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(timing ~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme 

# trim df to be one df for each type of parasite
acanths <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("acanthocephalan")) %>% 
  droplevels()

jk_a <- ggplot(jackknife_results,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")


nems <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("nematode")) %>% 
  droplevels()

jk_n <- ggplot(nems,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")

cest <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("cestode")) %>% 
  droplevels()

jk_c <- ggplot(cest,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")

trem <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("trematode")) %>% 
  droplevels() 

jk_t <- ggplot(trem,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")

trem_meta <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("trematode_metacercaria")) %>% 
  droplevels()

jk_tm <- ggplot(trem_meta,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")

mono <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("monogenean")) %>% 
  droplevels()

jk_mono <- ggplot(mono,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")

myxo <- jackknife_results %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("myxozoan")) %>% 
  droplevels()

jk_myxo <- ggplot(myxo,
       aes(x = timing, y = jackknife_mean_richness, fill = psite_spp_y)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Fish species",
    y = "Mean Parasite Richness (Jackknife)",
    fill = "Parasite type"
  ) + connor_theme + guides(fill = "none")

# combine all plots into one
biodiv_plot_hurricane <- ggarrange(jk_a, jk_n, jk_c, jk_t, jk_tm, jk_mono, jk_myxo, common.legend = FALSE, legend = "bottom", labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G"))
plot(biodiv_plot_hurricane)

```

# Troubleshooting
PROBLEMS TO FIX:
1) why do the raw data plots not match the model plots (trematode) (solved above)
```{r}
## remove elements of the model sequentially to see what happens to the model afterwards
# abundance 3 types
ha_nbinom2log3 <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance ~ event*timing*psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season),
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing*psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season), 
                                family = nbinom2, data = hurricane_df_abundance)


#### C
a1c <- ggpredict(ha_nbinom2log3, terms = c("psite_type", "event", "timing"))
a1c <- a1c %>%
  rename(
    psite_type = x,
    estimate = predicted,
    se = std.error,
    lower = conf.low,
    upper = conf.high,
    event = group, 
    timing = facet)

a1c <- a1c %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("trematode"))

ggplot(a1c,
       aes(x = timing, y = estimate)) +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, group = timing),
                width = 0.05,
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = psite_type, linetype = psite_type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = psite_type, group = psite_type),
             pch = 21, size = 5,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("trematode_metacercaria" = "#AFDFEF", "monogenean" = "#A6E000", "trematode" = "#c9ad79", "myxozoan" = "#07c3c5")) + 
  labs(x = "Timing", y = "Estimate", fill = "Parasite Type", linetype = "Parasite Type") +
  connor_theme

#RESULT:
# Removing fish_psite_combo and total_length_mm|fish_psite_combo and fish_sp_x:
# fish species is affecting the trend seen between the raw data and the predicted model, meaning that the high numbers of trematodes seen in hybnuc control are influencing the raw data (and are visible in the raw data) but when the model predictions hold this constant (as a random effect) that effect is insignificant.
```
now for metacercaria result (solved above too)
```{r}
## remove elements of the model sequentially to see what happens to the model afterwards
# abundance 3 types
ha_nbinom2log3 <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance ~ event*timing*psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season),
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing*psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season), 
                                family = nbinom2, data = hurricane_df_abundance)


#### C
a1c <- ggpredict(ha_nbinom2log3, terms = c("psite_type", "event", "timing"))
a1c <- a1c %>%
  rename(
    psite_type = x,
    estimate = predicted,
    se = std.error,
    lower = conf.low,
    upper = conf.high,
    event = group, 
    timing = facet)

a1c <- a1c %>% 
  filter(psite_type %in% c("trematode_metacercaria"))

ggplot(a1c,
       aes(x = timing, y = estimate)) +
  facet_wrap(~ event) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, group = timing),
                width = 0.05,
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = psite_type, linetype = psite_type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = psite_type, group = psite_type),
             pch = 21, size = 5,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("trematode_metacercaria" = "#AFDFEF", "monogenean" = "#A6E000", "trematode" = "#c9ad79", "myxozoan" = "#07c3c5")) + 
  labs(x = "Timing", y = "Estimate", fill = "Parasite Type", linetype = "Parasite Type") +
  connor_theme

# RESULT: For metas, the raw data could be emphasizing the metas in pimvig in control compared to hurricane
```

# Outdated 6/17/25

2) why are the myxo results between H and DF different
```{r, eval=FALSE}
# plot raw myxo data for hurricane
ggplot(hurricane_df_prevalence,
         aes(x = ci, y = psite_prevalence, fill = psite_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("myxozoan" = "#07c3c5")) + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1200), breaks = seq(0, 1200, by = 200)) +
  labs(x = "CI Category", y = "Parasite Prevalence", fill = "Parasite Type", linetype = "Parasite Type") +
  connor_theme 

# plot raw myxo data for DF
ggplot(drought_flood_df_m,
         aes(x = ci, y = psite_prevalence, fill = psite_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("myxozoan" = "#07c3c5")) + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1200), breaks = seq(0, 1200, by = 200)) +
  labs(x = "CI Category", y = "Parasite Prevalence", fill = "Parasite Type", linetype = "Parasite Type") +
  connor_theme 
```

```{r}
avg.bins.p <- hurricane_df_prevalence %>% 
  group_by(event, timing, psite_type, ci) %>% 
  summarize(avg.bin = mean(psite_prevalence))

# use this as the raw plot of data
ggplot(avg.bins.p,
       aes(x = ci, y = avg.bin)) +
  facet_wrap(timing ~ event) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = psite_type, group = psite_type),
             pch = 21, size = 5,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("trematode_metacercaria" = "#AFDFEF", "monogenean" = "#A6E000", "trematode" = "#c9ad79", "myxozoan" = "#07c3c5")) + 
  labs(x = "Timing", y = "Estimate", fill = "Parasite Type", linetype = "Parasite Type") +
  connor_theme
```
Various troubleshooting
FROM ARIAL -- WORKING ON ZI AND TESTING ASSUMPTIONS
```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
#### test 
ha_nbinom2log3_zi <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance2 ~ event*timing*psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_psite_combo) + (1|fish_sp_x) + (total_length_mm|fish_psite_combo),
                                family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = hurricane_df_abundance)

ha_nbinom2log3 <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance ~ event*timing + psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_psite_combo) + 
                                  (1|fish_sp_x) + (total_length_mm|fish_psite_combo),
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing*psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_psite_combo) + (1|fish_sp_x) +
                                  (total_length_mm|fish_psite_combo), 
                                family = nbinom2, data = hurricane_df_abundance)


ha_nbinom2log3_zi <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance2 ~ event*timing + psite_type + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_psite_combo) + (1|fish_sp_x) + (total_length_mm|fish_psite_combo),
                                family = binomial(link = "logit"), data = hurricane_df_abundance)

simulateResiduals(fittedModel = ha_nbinom2log3, plot = TRUE)
summary(ha_nbinom2log3)
plot(parameters(ha_nbinom2log3))


simulateResiduals(fittedModel = ha_nbinom2log3_zi, plot = TRUE)
summary(ha_nbinom2log3_zi)
plot(parameters(ha_nbinom2log3_zi))

levels(hurricane_df_abundance$psite_type)


#####calculating odds percentage from coefficients- [(exp(coef)-1)*100]
### ZI model (part one of hurdle)
#behavior (travel)
(exp(2.0029)-1)*100

##################### Isolating hurdle part 1 to test which is reference (0 or 1)
#add new column to create 0 and 1
hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance2 <- as.numeric(hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance)

#change non-zeros to 1
hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance2[hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance2>0] <- 1

#double check this worked and matches counts from original n_minute column --> counting up how many zeros are in the data
sum(hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance2>0)
sum(hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance2<1)

#change to factor so we can relevel
hurricane_df_abundance <- hurricane_df_abundance %>%
  mutate(psite_abundance2=as.factor(psite_abundance2))

levels(hurricane_df_abundance$psite_abundance2)

hurricane_df_abundance <- hurricane_df_abundance %>%
  mutate(psite_abundance2 = relevel(psite_abundance2, ref = "1"))

#### end test
```

SUMMARY NOTES:
- we have zero inflation 
- if the conditional estimate is positive, then the count is higher 
- if the zi estimate is positive, then you are expecting to see less ones and therefore more zeroes
- the zi model only concerns itself with either 0 or 1 for each observation --> all non zero values of count are converted to 1 (0 is our reference level, meaning we are seeking to understand what makes a parasite more likely to be found relative to not being found)
- UNANSWERED: does the conditional model contain zeros still, or are the zeros seperated out into the zi model (truncated removes all zeros from the conditional) --> I think yes, the zeros are still in the conditional model, the zi model just maps out the predictions of the probability of encountering a zero or not in a given sample given all factors
- do I want to do odds ratio calculations?
- META result I think can be explained by seasonality
- basically, the way that the controls in hurricane are set up makes it so that all control pre values are march - september when metas are more present and all control post are oct-march when they are less abundant, so we see a drop off between pre and post for control BUT hurricanes are much more variable in their pre and post (since control is the average of all hurricane times) and therefore the seasonal effect of metas is being buried in the analysis SO it looks like there is a drop off between control and hurricane but actually the main difference is between pre and post BUT what i am unsure about is, shouldnt the model with the element of timing pick out this relationship as significant? or is that potentially something buried in the estimate, since the model is using the reference level of pre and control to judge everything else? 
- OTHER Q: should we make all non control and non hurricane values into other control values? this is 1588 observations we are losing

Metas and temperature -- does warmer temperature mean more metacercaria?
```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
nbinom1sqrtoff <- glmmTMB(psite_abundance ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + 
                                  (1|fish_sp_x) + offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)) + (1|mean_temperature),
                                ziformula = ~ event*timing + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_x) + 
                                  offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)) + (1|mean_temperature), 
                                family = nbinom1(link = "sqrt"), data = hatm)

simulateResiduals(fittedModel = nbinom1sqrtoff, plot = TRUE)
summary(nbinom1sqrtoff)
plot(parameters(nbinom1sqrtoff))
```

Run df analyses with drought database
```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
### prevalence nbinom1sqrtoff
nbinom1sqrtoff <- glmmTMB(psite_prevalence ~ df_event + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + 
                                  (1|fish_sp_x) + offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)),
                                ziformula = ~ df_event + ci + (1|catalog_number) + (1|year_collected/season) + (1|fish_sp_x) + 
                                  offset(sqrt(total_length_mm)), 
                                family = nbinom1(link = "sqrt"), data = hurricane_df_prevalence)

simulateResiduals(fittedModel = nbinom1sqrtoff, plot = TRUE)
summary(nbinom1sqrtoff)
plot(parameters(nbinom1sqrtoff))
```


MYXO RESULTS DIFFER FROM HURRICANE TO DF ANALYSIS
- the sample sizes are different (there are 724 fewer observations in the hurricane df than there are in the drought/flood df)
- when I run thedf analysis using the hurricane dataset, the significance of the ciImpact goes from p < 0.001 to = 0.0043, when I run the optimized model parameterization for the DF database (picking the model that has not necessarily the lowest AIC, but low AIC and also good dHarma) the significance of ciImpact goes down to 0.08
- interpretation of this is that there is no significant relationship between CI and myxozoan prevalence in a hurricane or a drought, even though by just using the hurricane df we do see a significant relationship
- maybe we dont have enough power to be detecting the effect in the hurricane df, but we do in the DF one? 
- I also think that the parameterization and LACK of a interactive term in the DF model might be changing these results from H to DF, but I don't know which of those is more important
